title: TOP Open House Scripts to Covert Visitors Into Clients
excerpt: Open houses aren’t just for showing homes—they’re for building relationships. Yet too many agents waste these opportunities by fumbling through awkward small talk or missing…
content:
Open houses aren’t just for showing homes—they’re for building relationships. Yet too many agents waste these opportunities by fumbling through awkward small talk or missing key moments to engage serious buyers. Have you ever had visitors walk in, look around, and leave without a word? Without the right scripts, you’re losing leads and potential deals.

A strong open house script gives you confidence, keeps conversations flowing, and helps you collect valuable contact info—so you can turn casual lookers into real buyers. Ready to level up your open house game and stop letting leads slip through the cracks? Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

What Are Open House Scripts
Greeting Visitors
Addressing Buyer Concerns
Best Practices for Scripting
Frequently Asked Questions

What Are Open House Scripts

Open house scripts are guided conversations that provide real estate agents with the right wording to use with open house visitors. Consider these scripts to be your open house GPS, leading you successfully through every turn. This is why having great, effective scripts is so important.

Most importantly, it allows you to customize your approach when engaging with various types of visitors, from first-time homebuyers to experienced investors. This flexibility often results in faster time to clients and improved client follow-up. Good scripts read like a conversation, putting people at ease, and allowing you to establish rapport with potential buyers, making them feel comfortable and important.

Implementing open house scripts will remove the fear of first contact, increasing your confidence with any outcome. They’re great for testing interest, fielding tough questions, and making sure you collect critical contact info so you don’t miss out on a follow-up.

Scripts provide a time-efficient approach to prospecting so you can make many more calls in less time. Digital sign-in sheets are an even easier way to add leads directly into your database.

Greeting Visitors

Creating a welcoming atmosphere at an open house starts with a few essential elements:

  • A friendly smile and eye contact
  • An engaging tone to set a comforting mood
  • Personalized greetings using visitor names when possible
  • An invitation to explore the property freely

1. Start with a Warm Welcome

Start with a personal introduction, including your name and position. Invite visitors to submit inquiries or express what they would like to see featured.

Open gestures, smiling, nodding, and leaning forward all communicate approachability. Providing drinks or light refreshments adds to the atmosphere of friendliness and openness.

2. Set Up an Information Station

An information station should have pamphlets, property sheets, and business cards at minimum. A simple voluntary sign-up sheet goes a long way in collecting contact information.

Share neighborhood highlights and market trends to reel them in. Graphically design everything to be eye-catching, generating interest and excitement that leads to follow through.

3. Conduct an Initial Friendly Meeting

Engage with visitors and show true empathy for their home search. Follow-up with open-ended questions in order to figure out what they need.

Then really listen, to earn their trust. Keep meetings fun to make a great first impression.

Engaging Potential Buyers

1. Explore Search Criteria

Getting to the bottom of what potential buyers need is integral to really connecting with them and keeping their attention while showing an open house. It can be useful to have a prepared list of questions to draw out what their priorities are in a search. Start by determining which neighborhoods or areas they are looking for specifically.

Next, discuss their ideal bedroom and bathroom count, as well as must-have home or community amenities. Here’s a list of factors to consider. Identifying any special needs such as accessibility or yard space is essential. This knowledge doesn’t just leave you better-informed; it helps focus subsequent property recommendations and improve follow-up conversations.

By using open house scripts, you know you’re going to hit all the key points and do so with confidence. This strategy leaves an impressive first impression, making serious buyers out of tire kickers.

2. Paint a Future Vision

Painting a colorful picture of daily life in the house and community draws in those potential buyers. Share exciting narratives about living in the area, describing community events, local amenities, and lifestyle benefits.

Use descriptive language to get them picturing what it’ll be like to host parties or spend quality time with their family in the new space. This storytelling technique creates an authentically personal bond and puts them in a comfortable place. Scripts take the guesswork out of these discussions, alleviating pressure and increasing confidence no matter the scenario.

3. Assess Wishlist Items

A deep dive into what today’s buyers are looking for in a home is imperative. Create a wishlist item scorecard. List them all from big kitchen to big yard, then put the most important ones first.

By doing this, it allows them to focus their search on properties that fit their needs. Coming back to these items in follow-up conversations demonstrates that you are listening and paying attention to their needs.

Even if potential buyers aren’t showing interest at first, they can still turn into qualified leads, making your next sale possible. After all, nearly 80% of homebuyers start their search online. Make sure you’re prepared to respond to objections, like yard size and neighborhood features, in a positive manner.

Handling Casual Lookers

When you’re faced with casual lookers at an open house, the trick is to make the conversation without being pushy. Here’s a list of strategies to help with that:

  • Use casual fun discussion starters to gauge serious interest.
  • Share important information about the property while allowing them to discover it on their own.
  • Encourage questions and let them know you are available to assist them.

1. Check-In with Neighbors

Begin by greeting prospective neighbors with a friendly grin and a casual come hither. Share insights about neighborhood benefits and recent developments.

Invite them to share their story, which not only builds rapport with the audience but helps capture leads from those who may know someone looking to buy.

2. Engage Curiosity Seekers

When people are only lookie-looing, don’t fight it—meet them where they’re at. Provide them with cool, fun, or strange facts to get them intrigued and get them asking questions.

Turn their curiosity to your advantage by drawing attention to the home’s best attributes.

3. Inspire with Ideas

Provide inventive ideas for customizing the area or future improvements. Share success stories of buyers who renovated completed similar homes and benefited from the same.

Help visitors imagine themselves and their aesthetic in the house.

Following Up After Open Houses

Treating these follow-up steps as a core component of each open house is the start of turning more leads into clients. Here’s a bullet list of strategies to keep the momentum moving in the right direction:

  • Thank attendees for visiting and invite them to ask any questions.
  • Schedule follow-up calls within a day to get your new prospects’ thoughts on the property and discuss next steps.
  • Send them other properties or information that match what they’ve said they’re looking for.
  • Follow up through multiple channels phone, email, text, social media.
  • Follow up with a short text message after the call thanking them again and letting them know you’re excited to move forward.

1. Extend an Open Invitation

Make sure your communication lines are open for any questions or future showings. Extending an offer to schedule private showings for properties meeting their criteria helps leave the door ajar.

Remind them to call you when they have friends or relatives in the market for a home, too — this is a good way to stay in touch. Keeping the tone friendly might make the next communications more friendly as well.

2. Provide Real Estate Expertise

Helping them understand today’s market conditions and pricing trends will make you a trusted source and authority. Providing useful advice about how to approach the home-buying journey while showing your years of wisdom establishes trust and authority.

Keep in mind that 80% of sales are made between the 5th and 12th contact, so diligent follow-up is essential.

Addressing Buyer Concerns

Making your open house a welcoming environment starts with addressing buyer concerns before they even walk through the door. Here’s a bullet list of common concerns to address proactively:

  • Price and affordability
  • Condition of the property
  • Neighborhood safety and amenities
  • Potential for future value increase
  • Proximity to schools and workplaces
  • Commute times and public transportation options

Being able to listen closely to concerns or objections in the middle of a call or meeting is incredibly important. Giving a direct and forthright answer quickly takes the wind out of their concerns or confusion.

Address buyer concerns. Show buyers that you’re committed to helping them find their ideal home, and you’ll win their trust by demonstrating empathy and understanding.

Share Neighborhood Insights

Talking about local amenities, schools, parks and community events further paints a picture that makes living in the area highly desirable. Data on safety and demographic breakdowns help to instill confidence.

Showcase testimonials from existing residents focusing on the happy moments. Emphasize positive future changes that will add to appreciation.

Discuss House Condition Queries

Be ready to discuss the home’s age, maintenance history, and any repairs that have been done. Show your strengths, but don’t hide the ball—be honest about weaknesses.

Provide any available documentation about the property’s condition. Incentivize buyers to look at homes with improvement or renovation potential.

Turning Neighbors into Leads

Engaging neighbors when they attend an open house is a savvy tactic for converting them into a future lead. Here are some strategies:

  • Share details about the property’s features and benefits.
  • Provide individual tours around the open house to field questions.
  • Highlight the neighborhood’s unique selling points.

1. Offer Market Value Discussions

Having honest conversations about your local market can be a difference maker. Providing information on recent sales and their impact on local property values can create a buzz.

This type of simple conversation makes you sound like the wise old real estate guru.

As the date approaches, reach out to homeowners to remind them of the Open House, targeting a three to four block radius from your listing.

Make sure you stay in touch with them post-event, because turning a first-meeting handshake into a relationship can unlock many opportunities. Yet only 4% of agents send them, creating a huge opportunity.

Collecting Visitor Information

Collecting visitor information at an open house is critical to developing a great contact list for future outreach. Here are some efficient methods to consider:

  • Implement digital sign-in methods such as tablets or mobile apps like Curb Hero to provide a more efficient experience.
  • Provide explicit directions on what information they should provide, like their names, emails, and phone numbers.
  • Offer something enticing such as raffle entries to encourage visitors to provide their contact details.
  • Protect the privacy of all data collected, making it clear to potential visitors why they should share their information.

Use Efficient Sign-In Methods

A digital sign-in app is quicker and easier to use, which means you’re collecting many more leads, including the “just browsing” ones. Syncing these leads with a real estate CRM allows you to follow up quickly and make the most of every visitor.

Keeping an eye on sign-in trends allows us to more effectively adjust our efforts moving forward.

Learn About Attendee Interests

Consider questions like the following to dig deep and learn more about what visitors really need and their timeframes. Use this information to customize follow-ups and recommend properties that best match their expressed interests.

This approach increases your chances of making successful connections.

Objection Handling Strategies

Address Common Buyer Objections

The key to handling buyer objections at your open house is preparation and empathy. Here’s a bullet list of common objections and effective responses to address them:

  • Show them what the market is doing and provide apples-to-apples comps.
  • Promote the positive features of the community, like good schools or nearby parks.
  • Propose modifications or similar options in the area if the existing property doesn’t fit their needs.

When you know what everyone’s worried about, it makes it way easier to turn objections into opportunities. Use empathetic listening to validate their feelings, giving them reassurance that you will always have their best interests in mind.

Share objective data, such as sales data from the prior year, and use it to make your case while fostering a conversation. This strategy makes buyers feel heard and understood, which builds trust. Sending along further information, such as market intelligence reports, serves to further establish your in-depth knowledge of the industry.

Best Practices for Scripting

Creating effective open house scripts involves several key practices:

  • Keep messages concise to focus on essential details.
  • Use natural language that reflects your personal style.
  • Regularly update scripts based on feedback and market trends.

Maintain a Conversational Tone

A conversational tone is very important. This is where you can use humor or relatable stories to help lighten the mood.

Don’t use jargon that will lose your audience. The idea is to develop connections, not make the hard sale.

Adapt to Different Situations

Here’s a bullet list of situations where change is needed:

  • Serious buyers vs. casual lookers
  • Unexpected questions or comments

Be flexible and adjust based on visitor comfort.

Emphasize Key Benefits

Make sure to call out standout property features and how they align with buyer needs. Concentrate on making sure they are working with you as their buyer’s agent.

Avoid jargon, and use engaging, persuasive language to drum up excitement.

Key Takeaways

  • Open house scripts are a must-have for real estate agents. They help set the tone in person with visitors and increase visitor client conversion and lead nurturing chances.
  • We’re glad to have you here at Open House! Looking forward to meeting you there! First, just allow me to introduce myself, and then get you settled with some drinks and snacks.
  • Have an information station with brochures, sign-up sheets, and knowledge of the ‘hood. This will make it easier for visitors to gather key information and motivate them to take action.
  • Engaging potential buyers involves asking open-ended questions, using storytelling to paint a vision of life in the home, and tailoring discussions to their preferences.
  • For casual lookers, use light conversation starters and provide property information without pressure, inviting questions to keep them engaged.
  • Following up after open houses with personalized emails and calls can convert leads into clients, while addressing buyer concerns with honesty and expertise builds trust.

That’s a Wrap!

A strong open house script is the difference between a forgettable event and a lead-generating machine. When you know what to say, how to engage visitors, and how to follow up, you’ll turn more casual lookers into serious buyers. Be confident, ask the right questions, and keep the conversation flowing—because every open house is an opportunity to grow your business!

Frequently Asked Questions

What Are Open House Scripts?

Open house scripts are rehearsed conversations for real estate agents. They provide a reference to focus conversations with visitors during open houses. These scripts help to make sure agents are getting important topics covered and that they’re being professional.

How Do You Greet Visitors at an Open House?

Welcome your visitors with a friendly smile and address them by name. Collect their names and thank them for coming. This creates a friendly atmosphere and invites participation.

How Can You Engage Potential Buyers?

Engage them with open-ended questions around their home needs and wants. Draw attention to features of the property that align with their interests. This continues to build rapport and more importantly, keeps buyers engaged and interested.

How Should You Handle Casual Lookers?

Keep interactions courteous and educational but don’t pressure them. Provide a high-level description and encourage them to learn on their own time. This approach honors their current interest level while leaving the door open for them to reengage down the line.

Why Is Following Up Important After Open Houses?

Even more importantly, following up keeps you top of mind and demonstrates your professionalism. It’s a great opportunity to clear up any last-minute questions and it keeps your property in the forefront of their minds. This often results in serious, qualified offers from buyers who are ready to commit.

What Are Best Practices for Scripting?

Please make sure scripts are realistic and appropriate for an open house audience. Get ready to pivot and change course based on how visitors react. Customize provided scripts to show off your property’s distinctive features. This methodology produces the best communication and the best experience for the visitor.

title: How Curbio Renovation Services Operate Within eXp Realty
excerpt: Discover how eXp Realty agents boost listings with Curbio’s pay-on-close renovations—no project management required.
content:
Key Takeaway: Curbio is an optional eXp Realty solution that provides pay-at-close, fully managed pre-listing renovations. The program focuses on cosmetic updates and handles contractor coordination, materials, and timelines, allowing sellers to complete renovations without upfront payment and agents to avoid managing renovation logistics.

TL;DR About Curbio at eXp Realty

  • Curbio is optional, not required
  • Sellers pay renovation costs at closing, not upfront
  • Designed to support cosmetic pre-listing updates prior to market
  • Project management, contractors, and timelines handled by Curbio
  • Available in many major U.S. markets
  • Part of the broader eXp Realty Solutions ecosystem

Preparing a home for sale often involves updates that sellers may not have the time, cash, or desire to manage. Pre-listing renovation programs are sometimes used to address cosmetic issues that affect presentation, without requiring sellers or agents to coordinate contractors, materials, or payments in advance.

This article explains how Curbio fits into the broader eXp Realty Solutions ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your index:

Table of Contents

TL;DR About Curbio at eXp Realty
How Some eXp Realty Agents Use Curbio
Curbio Availability And Market Coverage
How Agents Evaluate Curbio For Listing Preparation
How Renovation Preparation Can Affect Listing Strategy
How Pre-Listing Updates Fit Into Long-Term Listing Strategies
Curbio Benefits for Home Sellers
How Curbio Differs From Traditional Contractor Coordination
What Agents Also Ask About Curbio
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Solutions Guides

How Some eXp Realty Agents Use Curbio

Curbio is an optional eXp Realty Solution that provides pre-listing renovation services designed to support listing preparation workflows. Rather than functioning as a general contractor for major structural projects, Curbio focuses on defined cosmetic updates that are commonly addressed prior to listing, such as interior paint, flooring replacement, kitchen and bathroom refreshes, and exterior curb appeal improvements.

These renovations are selected based on scope guidelines and market considerations, with project management handled by Curbio. Agents often evaluate this approach as a way to address visible condition issues and standardize listing readiness without managing contractors or renovation logistics directly.

Curbio Availability And Market Coverage

Curbio operates nationwide through its exclusive partnership with eXp Realty, making it available to agents in most U.S. cities. This widespread availability ensures that eXp agents can confidently offer pre-sale home improvements to sellers, knowing they have a trusted partner to handle every aspect of the process.

How The Pre-Listing Renovation Process Works

From the moment an agent submits a project request, the process is seamless. A team of pre-listing experts conducts an in-home consultation to determine the most valuable updates. There’s no guesswork—every recommendation is backed by data to maximize a seller’s return on investment. By focusing on upgrades that buyers actually want, Curbio helps eXp agents ensure that their listings stand out in the market.

End-to-End Project Management for Agents

Once the scope of work is approved, a dedicated project manager takes over. They handle everything—coordinating vetted contractors, sourcing materials, overseeing timelines, and sending real-time updates through Curbio’s app. This level of support means eXp agents never have to deal with subcontractor scheduling, permit tracking, or unexpected delays. Everything is managed through Curbio’s intuitive platform, providing a completely hands-off experience for agents and their sellers. With Curbio in place, agents can focus on selling homes rather than managing renovations

How Agents Evaluate Curbio For Listing Preparation

For eXp agents, Curbio is a game-changer. The ability to offer sellers a fully managed, pay-at-close renovation solution makes an agent more than just a transaction facilitator—it positions them as a trusted advisor. By eliminating the upfront cost barrier for sellers, agents ensure homes are market-ready without financial roadblocks. This service alone sets eXp agents apart, transforming the way they attract and close listings.

How Renovation Preparation Can Affect Listing Strategy

Curbio provides an undeniable competitive edge. While other agents are scrambling to coordinate handymen and contractors, eXp agents using Curbio can promote “sell-ready homes” with professionally managed updates and no out-of-pocket expenses for the seller. This can help turn leads into clients. With Curbio’s nationwide network, agents can take on properties in any condition, knowing that renovations will be expertly handled.

Scaling Without the Hassle

With Curbio managing every aspect of the pre-sale renovation process, eXp agents can confidently take on more listings without stretching themselves too thin. Whether a home needs a quick refresh or a full-scale cosmetic update, agents know they have a reliable partner to get the job done efficiently and effectively. This allows them to focus on growing their business rather than managing the complexities of home improvement projects.

How Pre-Listing Updates Fit Into Long-Term Listing Strategies

Beyond just selling homes, Curbio helps agents future-proof their business. Buyers increasingly expect move-in-ready homes, and outdated listings struggle to compete. With Curbio, agents ensure that their listings align with current market demands, helping them close deals faster and more profitably. The impact extends beyond a single transaction—sellers appreciate the ease of the process, leading to more referrals and a reputation as the agent who delivers results without hassle.

Curbio Benefits for Home Sellers

Sellers also gain significant advantages from Curbio’s model, making the home-selling process smoother, more profitable, and stress-free. By eliminating upfront renovation costs and providing full project management, Curbio helps sellers maximize their home’s value while minimizing hassle.

Financial Flexibility Without Upfront Costs

Curbio allows sellers to complete approved renovations without paying upfront, with renovation costs settled at closing from sale proceeds. Interest may apply during the renovation period, and sellers should review program terms to understand total costs and eligibility before proceeding.

A Stress-Free Home Improvement Experience

Curbio also eliminates the stress that typically comes with preparing a home for sale. Homeowners no longer have to worry about managing multiple contractors, tracking down materials, or navigating renovation timelines. With a dedicated project manager overseeing everything from start to finish, sellers can focus on their next move while Curbio ensures their home is market-ready.

Faster Turnaround for a More Competitive Listing

Efficiency is key in today’s fast-moving market, and Curbio helps sellers get their homes listed without delay. The streamlined project management process ensures that renovations don’t drag on, reducing the risk of a property sitting on the market and losing value.

How Curbio Differs From Traditional Contractor Coordination

Curbio has reimagined home improvement to more effectively serve the needs of real estate. They give agents and their clients a simple, reliable solution. By keeping a focus on efficiency and results, Curbio makes sure that every renovation goes straight to improving the home sale process.

Here’s how Curbio stands out:

Clear and Transparent Communication Throughout the Process

Transparency is at the core of Curbio’s philosophy. The entire renovation process is managed through their app, providing frequent updates that keep both agents and sellers fully informed at every stage. This level of communication fosters trust, ensures that everyone stays on the same page, and eliminates unnecessary stress.

Trusted Partner for Real Estate Agents

More importantly, Curbio has proven itself as a reliable solution for thousands of real estate professionals across the country. Their unique pay-at-closing model removes upfront renovations costs, so it’s easier for sellers to make needed improvements before listing.

Reliable and Professional Contractors

Curbio’s renovation as a service model takes the complexity out of the renovation process. They take care of sourcing materials to coordinating schedules, making sure projects are moving along efficiently and professionally.

What Agents Also Ask About Curbio

Is using Curbio required for eXp Realty agents?
No. Curbio is an optional solution within eXp Realty Solutions. Agents may choose to use Curbio for certain listings, work with other renovation vendors, or list properties as-is. Participation has no impact on commission splits, caps, sponsorship, or standing with eXp Realty.

How does Curbio compare to hiring contractors directly?
Curbio differs by providing a fully managed, pre-listing renovation service with pay-at-close funding. Unlike hiring contractors directly, agents and sellers do not manage bids, timelines, materials, or payments, and the renovation scope is designed specifically to maximize resale value.

Who is Curbio best suited for?
Curbio is best suited for sellers who want to improve their home before listing but lack upfront cash, time, or desire to manage renovations. It is commonly used for cosmetic updates that improve marketability rather than structural or custom remodel projects.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Curbio is designed to address one of the most common listing challenges agents face, preparing homes for sale when sellers lack time, cash, or desire to manage renovations, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do eXp Realty agents have to use Curbio for renovations?
No. Curbio is completely optional. eXp Realty agents may recommend Curbio, use other renovation providers, or list homes without updates. Using or not using Curbio does not affect an agent’s brokerage relationship, compensation structure, or access to other eXp Realty solutions.

Who pays for Curbio renovations and when?
Renovation costs are paid by the seller at closing from sale proceeds. There are no upfront payments required. Interest is charged only during the renovation period, and the full balance is settled once the transaction closes.

Does Curbio handle project management and contractors?
Yes. Curbio manages the entire renovation process, including contractor coordination, materials, timelines, and updates. Agents and sellers do not manage subcontractors or scheduling, which allows agents to remain focused on listing preparation and marketing.

Are there limits on the types of renovations Curbio performs?
Yes. Curbio focuses on high-impact, cosmetic updates such as paint, flooring, kitchens, bathrooms, and curb appeal. Structural changes, additions, or custom remodels are generally outside the scope of Curbio’s pre-listing renovation model.

Is Curbio available in every market?
No. Curbio operates in most major U.S. markets, but availability varies by location. Agents should confirm coverage for their specific market before recommending Curbio to a seller, as service areas continue to expand over time.

title: Door Knocking in Real Estate: Turn Cold Doors to Hot Leads
excerpt: Let’s be real—door knocking in real estate can be intimidating. The thought of interrupting someone’s day, facing rejection, or stumbling over your words keeps many…
content:
Let’s be real—door knocking in real estate can be intimidating. The thought of interrupting someone’s day, facing rejection, or stumbling over your words keeps many agents from trying it. But avoiding it means missing out on one of the most personal, cost-effective ways to generate leads.

Want to stand out from the sea of online agents? A strong door-knocking strategy can help you build trust, start real conversations, and uncover hidden opportunities that digital marketing simply can’t reach. The key? Confidence, preparation, and the right approach. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

Why Door Knocking Works
Essential Tools for Door Knocking
That’s a Wrap!

Why Door Knocking Works

Advantages of Personal Interaction

There’s simply no substitute for the kind of face-to-face interactions that only real estate can provide to help foster great relationships. Meeting homeowners directly allows for genuine conversations, where both parties can engage meaningfully. This method is particularly powerful when compared to the often impersonal feel of digital communication.

When you’re physically in front of a homeowner, being able to use your body language and tone of voice can really help build that rapport. These non-verbal cues go a long way in communicating your genuine interest and breaking you out of the pack of competitors who default to email or phone.

With limited investment, the unique and personal touch of door knocking gives you a low-cost marketing strategy that truly stands out and works.

Building Trust with Homeowners

Door knocking is a great way for real estate agents to demonstrate their authenticity and trustworthiness. Providing local market knowledge and expertise helps build credibility with homeowners. By sharing personal stories, you add a layer of relatability that builds trust.

Following up on that initial talk shows that you’re trustworthy and serious, making them trust you even more. This approach is particularly important given that 80% of homebuyers want agents who have specific knowledge about the area they want to move to.

Creating Immediate Connections

Starting conversations with open-ended questions builds rapport and allows residents to feel like they are listened to. Using active listening techniques to demonstrate authentic curiosity about their issues helps to establish a comfortable environment.

This kind of environment encourages homeowners to provide their feedback candidly, so you can receive the most insightful critiques. These kinds of insights can be applied to improve your services and target your marketing efforts, strengthening your position as the local expert even more.

Being known as a credible professional who cares for the community will garner you more word of mouth referrals and business opportunities. Door knocking success is not simply closing a deal. It’s about nurturing those budding relationships, even those with the individuals who aren’t prepared to purchase or sell today.

Preparing for Door Knocking

Addressing door knocking in real estate means being strategic. Having a thorough plan is important. You’ll need to identify strategic priority neighborhoods and even individual homes to door-knock. The more familiar you are with your territory the better.

Know your neighborhood demographics to plan your approach. Wealthy family neighborhoods may at least partially be buffered with a parent at home in the afternoon. Don’t just assume that evenings or weekends are going to be the best times. They may very well be. Instead, drive around to find FSBO signs, or look for expired listings ahead of time.

Conduct Thorough Research

Understanding property values and trends is critical. This can help identify potential leads. Collect detailed information on comparable recent sales and nearby amenities.

Best of all, it arms you with helpful talking points once you’re door knocking with homeowners. In addition, look into events in the community. Finding mutual interests can help establish trust.

Gather Necessary Supplies

Put together a folder of all the things you’ll need including flyers, business cards, or door hangers. Personalized, meaningful gifts or notes can go a long way and have a lasting impact.

Have a sure-fire method for logging interactions. It helps with follow-up, too.

Dress Appropriately and Comfortably

Professional, comfortable clothing is a must. Showcase your brand, but be mindful of neighborhood culture. Things like a clean appearance go a long way to inspire confidence.

Set Clear Objectives

Have clear, concrete objectives for each session. Getting new leads or booking more appointments are a few.

Develop specific, measurable outcomes so you can measure success. Change goals based on what you learned.

Effective Door Knocking Strategies

1. Plan Your Approach

Having a door knocking game plan is key. Door knocking can sometimes feel chaotic. Begin by developing a simple, step-by-step plan for each stop. Work out the central talking points that will appeal to homeowners and outline possible pushback.

Be prepared with answers to alleviate these fears. First, plan your visits at times where you know you’ll have the best chance of finding homeowners, such as in the morning. Setting an achievable cap on how many homes you can hit in a day will save you from burning out and help you maintain your enthusiasm.

2. Start with a Friendly Greeting

Sometimes, though, a nice smile and friendly attitude go a long way. Addressing the homeowner by name is important. Using the homeowner’s full name to introduce yourself will further increase the degree of personalization.

If you live in the neighborhood, let them know so you can get them to feel like you’re a friend and neighbor, not a scary stranger. This friendly introduction opens the door to trust and transparency.

3. Understand Client Needs

Asking the right questions is the secret sauce to learning what motivates and worries your homeowners. By carefully listening, you’ll be able to customize your pitch to address their unique needs, making the value of your service more relevant.

Invite them in, do some research, ask questions, show understanding and concern for their situation and ultimately you’ll earn their trust and win more listings.

4. Develop a Persuasive Pitch

Creating a short, clear, and engaging message is key. Identify your unique value proposition and employ narrative storytelling strategies to make your ask personal, timely, and relevant.

Be sure to highlight the features of your services that will best help the homeowner, clearing a path to making your offer stick out, and stand out.

5. Schedule Follow-Up Meetings

Suggest exact dates and times for future meetings to facilitate buy-in. Be courteous and professional, and include contact details so they are easily found.

This method keeps the line of communication open and makes it more likely you will convert.

6. Present Tailored Proposals

Tailor your proposals accordingly to what the homeowner says they are interested in. Provide compelling data and insights to make your case, presenting them with the right solutions to help them meet their own objectives.

A well-tailored proposal demonstrates understanding and increases trust.

7. Address Concerns Confidently

Recognize the objections that will come at you and prepare measured responses. Come prepared with evidence and examples to counter fears and build trust.

If you do encounter hesitations, keeping a cool, courteous attitude will not only diffuse any worries but ensure the chat stays friendly.

8. Close the Deal Successfully

Highlight the main takeaways and remind them of the value your services can provide. In each case, help motivate homeowners to act with clear next steps.

Leave with thanks. Thank them for their time, for their consideration, and to create a friendly climate for continued engagement.

9. Ensure Post-Sale Follow-Up

Follow-up calls or visits keep the relationship strong after the sale. Handwritten thank-you notes or small tokens of appreciation can go a long way and help keep prospective homeowners abreast of market changes and new listings.

This kind of continued communication helps to cement the relationship and is likely to result in repeat business or referrals down the road.

Adapting Techniques for Luxury Markets

Research High-End Neighborhoods

To get a read on the dynamics at play in affluent areas, begin by looking for telltale signs. Seek out gourmet restaurants, exclusive boutiques, and top-tier schools that’ll lure in luxury buyers.

Compare recent sales to identify pricing trends and changes in demand, giving insight into how homes in these neighborhoods are being valued. Knowing the local businesses and services that cater to affluent clients builds your credibility and rapport with affluent buyers.

This preparation enables brokers to provide perspectives that appeal to savvy luxury markets.

Build Strong Relationships

Deepening relationships with homeowners through regular communication is key. Showing up to local events helps build rapport and creates a sense of awareness of you and your business within the community.

Delivering content that luxury homeowners find valuable, such as market trends, establishes you as a trustworthy source. Building trust by showcasing market knowledge can secure multi-million-dollar listings, a strategy proven by successful agents like Ben Bacal.

Make Memorable First Impressions

Having a focused and polished image is key. High-quality marketing materials reflect brand sophistication, while mindful body language and confident tones ensure professionalism.

This new approach works well for luxury clients, helping during face-to-face negotiations, especially in-person deal making, where body language often can affect the deal.

Prepare for Potential Rejections

A good attitude goes a long way towards accepting “no” with professionalism. Rehearsing answers to potential objections helps make these interactions respectful and civically-minded.

Seeing rejections as learning opportunities is a great way to refine techniques and make a stronger first impression next time.

Essential Tools for Door Knocking

Bring Marketing Materials

Door knocking is far more effective when equipped with the proper materials. Have a variety of promotional materials including flyers and business cards on hand. Door hangers are eye catching and help initiate conversations.

Create flashy pieces that display your identity well. Add details of your offerings and knowledge of the local market. Emphasize unique selling points.

Promote interesting aspects of the homes you’re showing, like a standout feature or the benefits of the home’s location. Think about providing virtual training sessions over platforms like Zoom so anyone can join from anywhere.

Use Effective Communication Skills

Good communication is the key to creating new opportunities. Follow up with active listening. Let homeowners tell you what they need.

Keeping good eye contact and an open body language will help you build rapport and connect. Adjust your approach to suit each resident. A strong script will help you open and close conversations with ease.

Invite them to meet one on one to talk about their specific needs and goals.

Offer Value to Residents

Establish yourself as a market expert by providing local context and knowledge. Give homeowners useful resources or information, such as home maintenance tips.

Feature community happenings or undertakings that would appeal to them. Building trust this way is a good way to list high-ticket items.

Door knocking is a low-cost brand strategy that can result in multi-million-dollar contracts.

Overcoming Challenges in Door Knocking

In real estate, door knocking does come with a few challenges. The first hurdle is sometimes the biggest: rejection. Maybe you knock on 100 doors to get 20 good conversations. To avoid these, it’s most important to think strategically.

Tools like PropertyRadar allow Realtors to target the neighborhoods that match their buyers’ needs, making the process more efficient. PropertyRadar’s rich data on properties and owners helps you get more targeted, with the right personalized messaging to break through to qualified clients.

Handle No Responses Gracefully

When no one’s home, dropping off branded materials ensures you remain on voters’ radar. Plus, this method is more respectful of homeowners’ privacy, making sure you aren’t an unwelcome surprise.

Keeping a good attitude helps too, because every door you don’t get an answer at is an opportunity to work on getting a better answer the next time.

Address Objections Effectively

If you can anticipate their objections, you’ll be able to prepare some thoughtful responses. Using data and testimonials to demonstrate that your program works goes a long way toward assuaging concerns.

Even if a door knocking conversation is unexpectedly difficult, staying cool shows that you are professional and worthy of trust.

Avoid Closing on Doorstep

Build rapport, not a sale. First impressions matter. Plan follow-ups to build bridges and strengthen ties.

This leaves the door open for future interactions, providing a non-pressured way to reconnect.

Best Practices for Successful Visits

Developing a schedule that blends door knocking with all your marketing activities is a recipe for success. This involves planning your visits for optimal times, such as weekdays between 8 a.m. 11:30 a.m., when people are more likely to be at home.

There’s no substitute for a strong first impression, and a well-prepared script can ensure you provide vital information in a clear, concise manner. With a good response rate of 20%, timing and approach make all the difference.

Limit Daily Door Knocks

By making a daily target of around 20 homes, advocates can have productive conversations without experiencing volunteer burnout. This simple number allows you to have real conversations, which are always better than an in-and-out visit.

Being mindful of your energy levels will help you stay excited and make every visit worthwhile.

Monitor Your Progress Regularly

Documenting your meetings allows you to measure impact. Monitoring what’s successful and what isn’t allows you to hone your strategy year after year.

Continuous improvement means celebrating small successes, which keeps motivation high and goals always in sight.

Implement Safety Measures

Safety must always come first. Let a trusted friend know where you’ll be, bring a fully charged phone, and use your judgment on what areas of the neighborhood are safe to be in.

Don’t venture into places that make you feel unsafe, for your own safety and peace of mind.

Enhancing Engagement with Residents

Door knocking in real estate is an art that calls for smooth, relatable, and personable engagement. One of the most effective tactics is to pose provocative questions. I like to design open-ended questions, like having homeowners tell me what it’s been like to live in the neighborhood.

This creates a safe space for them to share more personal insights while allowing me to better comprehend their needs. Discussing topics like their home’s unique features, neighborhood changes, or future plans often leads to meaningful conversations. These conversations shape the experience and build trust.

Offering sincere praise is another good strategy. One of my favorite things about a homeowners property is an expansive beautiful garden or a large covered porch. This fosters an inviting, friendly environment.

This level of detail conveys respect and appreciation, establishing a strong bond. Inviting new homeowners to community events is a great way to build connections. Providing a calendar of local events they could participate in is a great way to generate that initial interest and encourage further engagement.

These invitations are a great way to showcase the benefits of community engagement, further building the neighborhood’s sense of community spirit.

Key Takeaways

  • Door knocking in real estate is all about face-to-face interactions. It uses the power of face-to-face interactions to foster authentic relationships and trust with homeowners. This technique creates opportunities for instant feedback and more meaningful relationships that digital communication can’t provide.
  • Door knocking gives real estate professionals an opportunity to create authentic relationships. It makes their communication more human with body language and allows them to rise above competitors who only use digital ways.
  • Real estate agents can maximize their door knocking success by doing some extra leg work to get to know the neighborhoods they’re targeting. This allows them to further develop their strategic plans on a home by home basis.
  • When agents come ready with all the right supplies, including marketing materials and gifts, they ensure they make a strong impression on homeowners. This mindset increases their probability of getting favorable replies and future engagement opportunities.
  • It takes a strategy to be successful with door-knocking. You have to deeply plan, then implement your strategy with precision, then follow-up, all while adjusting to every neighborhood’s unique prevailing winds.
  • To address these door knocking challenges, you need to be resilient and flexible. View objections and rejections as great learning experiences that help you grow and improve.

That’s a Wrap!

Door knocking works—if you do it right. With the right mindset, strategy, and follow-up, you can turn cold introductions into warm leads and lasting relationships. It’s not about selling on the spot—it’s about building trust, offering value, and staying top-of-mind for when homeowners are ready to make a move. Knock with confidence, and the results will follow!

Frequently Asked Questions

What makes door knocking effective in real estate?

Door knocking fosters genuine relationships. It enables realtors to interact face-to-face with potential homeowners, helping to build a relationship based on trust and rapport. This personal touch can help agents stand out in a crowded market.

How should I prepare for door knocking?

Know as much as you can about the neighborhood and plan a route. Plan to dress like a professional and come prepared with relevant materials, such as business cards. Walk through your pitch to ensure you always come off confident and concise.

What are some effective door knocking strategies?

So be friendly and take time to listen. Make your pitch specific to what the homeowner is looking for. Make sure to follow up with a handwritten thank-you note or email to cement the relationship.

How can I adapt door knocking techniques for luxury markets?

Provide highly personalized service with an air of exclusivity. Show that you’re specialized in luxury properties and that you understand the special market’s unique needs.

What tools are essential for door knocking?

Use a good CRM to keep track of leads and follow-up. Have either a notepad or a tablet with you to take notes. Business cards, brochures, etc. Are great too.

How can I overcome challenges in door knocking?

Always expect rejection and be courteous regardless of the outcome. Be flexible and willing to change your approach based on what you hear. As with all things, a little persistence and adaptability go a long way.

What are best practices for successful door knocking visits?

Plan your canvassing times for when homeowners will be most likely to be home. Be mindful of their time, and never burn a bridge – always leave them wanting more.

title: Best Real Estate Companies to Work For By Commission Splits in 2026
excerpt: Ready to look for the best real estate companies to work for? Let’s get into the nitty-gritty of commission splits. We know choosing the right…
content:
Ready to look for the best real estate companies to work for? Let’s get into the nitty-gritty of commission splits. We know choosing the right brokerage is more than just a career move. It’s about finding the perfect fit for your income potential and job satisfaction. But, let’s face it, if you’re not earning enough, all the professional satisfaction in the world won’t pay the bills.

So how much will you make at different brokerages? We get it. Figuring out how much you’ll make at different brokerages can be a real headache, especially if you have to interview every single one. But don’t worry, we’ve got you covered! We’ve done the legwork, researching and interviewing to gather key information on a variety of brokerages.

In this article, we’ll break down the commission splits you can expect from some of the most popular and established brokerages. Whether you’re a seasoned agent or just starting, understanding these splits can make all the difference in maximizing your earnings.

Make no mistake, how you do at a brokerage requires more financial and non-financial comparisons than just your commission split.  But no worries. We’ll cover many of those other components in future blogs.  However, your commission split will most likely be the biggest contributor to your bottom line and overall happiness.  So we will start there. Here’s your handy dandy index:

How Commissions Work

Alright, now that we’ve set the stage, let’s break down how commissions work in real estate. First, it’s essential to understand the different types of commission structures and how the math plays out for each one.

Fixed Percentage Splits

This is the most straightforward model. The commission is split based on a fixed percentage agreed upon with your brokerage. Common splits include 50/50, 60/40 or 70/30.  We’ll break down exactly what that means in the next section.

Graduated Splits

Graduated splits adjust based on your performance or graduation from brand new agent status. For example:

  • You might start at a 50/50 split as a new agent.
  • After hitting certain sales targets or gaining experience, your split could improve to 60/40 or even higher.

Cap System

In the cap system, you contribute a percentage of your commissions to the brokerage until you reach a predetermined cap. After hitting this cap, you keep 100% of your commissions for the rest of the year. This can be a significant advantage for high-producing agents.

For instance, if a brokerage sets a cap at $16,000, once you’ve paid this amount, all subsequent commissions are yours to keep each year, maximizing your earnings.

100% Commission Plan

With a 100% commission plan, you don’t split your commission with the brokerage. Instead, you pay a flat fee per transaction or monthly office fees.

Royalty Fees

While not a commission split, royalty fees are an important factor to consider. All brokerages charge some type of administrative fees, but royalty fees are often higher and can’t be ignored. These fees can eat into your earnings, so it’s crucial to factor them in when comparing different brokerages.

For example, if your split is 70/30 with your broker and there’s a 6% royalty fee, the “real split” for you is 64/36. We’ll cover brokerage fees in more detail later, but for now, we’ll include royalty fees in the commission split discussion.

Commission Math

Now that you’re familiar with the types of commission splits, let’s get into how the commission splits are calculated. This math applies to the vast majority of brokerages.

Total Commission Calculation

An agent’s commission is typically a percentage of the sale price, as specified in the listing or buyer agent agreement. This percentage can vary widely, usually ranging from 1.5% to 3.5% for each agent involved—the seller’s agent and the buyer’s agent.  Here’s a straightforward example:

Example:

  • Home Sale Price: $1,000,000
  • Listing or Buyer Agent Agreement Fee: 3%

Agent Commission Calculation:

  • Agent Commission = Sale Price x Commission Rate
  • Agent Commission = $1,000,000 x 3% = $30,000

Splitting Agent Commission with Your Brokerage

Once you’ve earned the commission, the next step is splitting it with your brokerage based on your contract terms. Here’s how it breaks down:

Example:

  • Agent’s Share: 80%
  • Brokerage’s Share: 20%

Step 1: Calculate Agent Earnings:

  • Agent’s Earnings = Total Commission x Agent’s Share
  • Agent’s Earnings = $30,000 x 80% = $24,000

Step 2: Calculate Brokerage Earnings:

  • Brokerage’s Earnings = Total Commission x Brokerage’s Share
  • Brokerage’s Earnings = $30,000 x 20% = $6,000

Splitting Agent Commission with Your Team

Not all agents are part of a team, but many, especially new agents, prefer to start their careers within a team structure. Teams typically charge a percentage of an agent’s earnings in exchange for value-added services like leads, guidance, resources, and tools. Here’s how you calculate your net earnings after splitting with your team:

Example:

  • Agent’s Earnings: $24,000 (after brokerage split)
  • Team’s Share: 25%

Calculate Agent’s Net After Team Split:

  • Agent’s Net Earnings = Agent’s Earnings – Team’s Share
  • Agent’s Net Earnings = $24,000 x 75% = $18,000

Research Challenges

Before we dive into the list of brokerages and their typical commission splits, there are a few things we need to clear up.

Caveat 1: Traditional Brokerage Model Splits Often Vary by Office

Yep, you read that right! It’s nearly impossible to quote an exact commission split for traditional brokerages. These are your classic franchise, brick-and-mortar offices. And yes, that’s most brokerages out there!

Why is it so tricky? Well, each franchise office is owned and operated by different broker owners who are free to set whatever commission splits they want. So, you can’t just look up the commission split on the internet for a particular traditional brokerage.

Caveat 2: Traditional Brokerage Model Splits Often Vary by Agent

To make things even more complicated, different agents within the same traditional brokerage likely have different commission splits. These variations are based on factors like agent experience, success, or even their potential for success. And guess what? The criteria for these splits are usually known only to the broker owner. So, again, no easy internet lookup here.

Caveat Resolution: Solving the Traditional Brokerage Model Problem

Given the non-transparent nature of traditional brokerage models, we’ve done a ton of research and conducted interviews to come up with a range of what most traditional brokerages seem to offer their agents.

Cloud-Based Brokerage Model Commission Splits

But hey, there’s some even better news! When it comes to cloud-based brokerages, commission split information is often transparent—you can actually look it up online. Cloud-based models don’t sell franchise offices and typically don’t have physical offices. This setup means lower expenses, no franchise bloat, and no duplicated staff across offices, allowing them to offer higher commission splits than traditional models.

Best Real Estate Companies to Work For

Alright, let’s get down to business with our commission split comparison.

We’ll present the information starting from the lowest commission split ranges for agents to the best commission splits.

And, fyi, we’ve skipped the lowest possible commission splits – those that are for brand new agents. Why? Because those don’t affect all agents and they’re temporary.

Without further ado, here are the commission splits that most agents at these large established brokerages earn.

Redfin: 30/70 – 75/25

Redfin is a discount brokerage that charges lower fees to clients and pays lower splits to agents. However, the trade-off is that Redfin agents are salaried and can gain their own clients to reach the higher end of the split.

Corcoran: 50/50 – 70/30

A traditional brokerage with a 6% royalty fee. The splits here are fairly standard, but keep in mind that royalty fee will take a bite out of your earnings.

Douglas Elliman: 50/50 – 70/30

Another traditional brokerage, also with a 6% royalty fee. Similar to Corcoran, your splits will depend on your experience and success, but that royalty fee is something to consider.

Coldwell Banker: 55/45 – 90/10

A well-known traditional brokerage with royalty fees ranging from 5% to 6.5%. They offer higher potential splits, especially for experienced agents.

Berkshire Hathaway: 60/40 – 90/10

Berkshire Hathaway operates under a traditional model with a 6% royalty fee. They provide competitive splits that can go up to 90/10 for top-performing agents.

Better Homes & Gardens: 60/40 – 80/20

This traditional brokerage charges a 6% royalty fee. Their splits are solid, offering a good balance for most agents.

Compass: 60/40 – 92.5/7.5

A traditional hybrid brokerage with a lower 4% fee. Again, top performers earn the higher split less the 4% fee.

The Agency: 70/30 – 90/10

Operating under a traditional model with a 6% royalty fee, The Agency has been expanding a lot lately.

Sotheby’s: 70/30 – 90/10

Another traditional brokerage with a 6% royalty fee. Sotheby’s is a good choice for experienced agents looking for a prestigious brand.

RE/MAX: 60/40 – 95/5

RE/MAX is a traditional brokerage with a 5% royalty fee. They have some of the best splits in the business, potentially going up to 95/5.

Century 21: 70/30 – 92/8

This traditional brokerage has a cap of about $22.5K and charges an 8% fee. They offer good splits and a cap that can benefit high earners.

Keller Williams: 70/30 – 100/0

Keller Williams, a traditional brokerage with a cap of $21-30K, charges a 6% royalty fee until the cap is reached. They offer the potential for agents to keep 100% of their earnings after capping.

eXp Realty: 80/20 – 100/0

A cloud-based modern brokerage with a cap of $16K and no royalty fees. eXp Realty offers some of the highest potential earnings, especially appealing for agents looking for flexibility and tech-driven support.

Finding the Best Fit for Your Real Estate Career

Beyond your commission split, it’s essential to evaluate other factors like brokerage fees, support, training, resources, tools, and community. All these elements together will help you determine the best fit for you. Be sure to check out our other resources for more insights on these criteria.

Also, consider a brokerage’s potential for future growth, passive income opportunities, and longevity in a rapidly evolving industry facing legal challenges and disruptions.

And hey, if you’re at one of the brokerages mentioned and your split is different, drop us a line. We’ll update this information to keep it as accurate as possible.

 Frequently Asked Questions

  •  What are the different real estate commission split types?
  • What are real estate broker royalty fees?
  • How do I calculate my real estate commission?
  • How does an agent pay for their team?
  • What else does a real estate agent pay for besides brokerage and team splits?
  • Why can’t I easily find out a brokerage’s commission split?

 What are the different real estate commission split types?

Fixed Percentage Splits are based on a fixed percentage agreed upon with your brokerage. Common splits include 50/50, 60/40 or 70/30.  Graduated splits adjust based on your performance or graduation from brand new agent status. Cap System – here you contribute a percentage of your commissions to the brokerage until you reach a predetermined cap. After hitting this cap, you keep 100% of your commissions for the rest of the year. 100% Commission Plan – here you don’t split your commission with the brokerage. Instead, you pay a flat fee per transaction or monthly office fees.

What are real estate broker royalty fees?

Royalty fees are paid by agents who work at brokerages that are a franchise business. These fees can eat into your earnings, so it’s crucial to factor them in when comparing different brokerages. For example, if your split is 70/30 with your broker and there’s a 6% royalty fee, the “real split” for you is 64/36.

How do I calculate my real estate commission?

An agent’s commission is typically a percentage of the sale price, as specified in the listing or buyer agent agreement. Example: Home Sale Price: $1,000,000, Listing or Buyer Agent Agreement Fee: 3%, agent commission = $1,000,000 x 3% = $30,000. Next, the agent must split their commission with their brokerage. Example, in a 80/20 split, agent’s earnings = $30,000 x 80% = $24,000 and brokerage’s earnings = $30,000 x 20% = $6,000

How does an agent pay for their team?

Not all agents are part of a team, but many, especially new agents, prefer to start their careers within a team structure. Teams typically charge a percentage of an agent’s earnings in exchange for value-added services like leads, guidance, resources, and tools. Example: agent’s earnings: $24,000 (after brokerage split), Team’s Share: 25%. Agent’s Net Earnings = $24,000 x 75% = $18,000

What else does a real estate agent pay for besides brokerage and team splits?

They pay nearly all costs of doing business like license and association annual fees, marketing costs, support staff like editors and transaction coordinators, car expenses, office needs, all listing expenses and much more.

Why can’t I easily find out a brokerage’s commission split?

Traditional brokerages with franchises are owned and operated by different broker owners who are free to set whatever commission splits they want. To make things even more complicated, different agents within the same traditional brokerage likely have different commission splits too. Factors like agent experience, success, or even their potential for success lead the broker owner to offer different splits.

title: How eXp ICON Status Qualification Works for Agents
excerpt: Unlock the secrets to becoming an eXp Icon Agent quickly with our expert tips and exclusive insights to stand out in the program!
content:
Key Takeaway: eXp ICON Agent status rewards top-producing agents by reimbursing their annual cap in company stock. It does not change commission splits, but it converts production performance into ownership, making it a central component of how long-term income and wealth building work at eXp Realty.

TL;DR About eXp ICON Agent Status

  • ICON status is a production-based recognition program at eXp Realty
  • Qualifying agents earn their annual cap back in company stock
  • ICON does not change commission splits or caps
  • Stock awards are tied to production, culture, and event participation
  • ICON status converts expenses into ownership rather than cash bonuses
  • Participation is based on annual performance
  • ICON is part of the broader eXp Realty income model, not a standalone program

High-producing agents eventually reach a point where commission splits matter less than what happens after they cap. At eXp Realty, ICON Agent status is designed to reward sustained production by converting brokerage expenses into company ownership rather than recurring costs. This distinction is often misunderstood, especially by agents comparing brokerages only on headline splits.

ICON status does not replace commission income and it does not function as a bonus program. Instead, it sits within eXp Realty’s broader income structure, alongside capped commissions and stock awards, as a mechanism for long-term value creation.

This article explains how eXp ICON Agent status fits into the broader eXp Realty income ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

TL;DR About eXp ICON Agent Status
What is an eXp ICON Agent
Qualification Steps to Achieve eXp ICON Agent Status
Common Practices Used by Agents Working Toward ICON Status
Understanding the eXp ICON Agent Stock Awards
What Agents Also Ask About eXp ICON Agent Status
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Income Guides

What is an eXp ICON Agent

Definition and Overview

An eXp ICON Agent is a high-producing agent recognized annually for meeting defined production, cultural, and participation requirements. In short, they’re recognized for providing an extraordinary level of productivity and service to the eXp Realty community.

Only agents who achieve certain thresholds are eligible to earn this prestigious award. It represents the highest level of achievement and is in complete harmony with eXp Realty’s core values. These agents are part of the elite, top 1% of agents across the world.

They are excited about eXp Realty because they can not only get stock awards, but they get to recover their $16,000 cap in the form of EXPI stock. Naturally, the ICON Agent Award has become a favorite since it was created in 2015, and for good reason.

Role and Responsibilities

The role of an eXp ICON Agent goes far beyond selling. These elite agents give back by mentoring their peers, creating a culture of encouragement, dedication, and support that propels everyone forward.

They maintain an extraordinary level of ethics, and professionalism shines through on every deal. eXp ICON agents have a direct impact on eXp Realty’s culture through their involvement in initiatives and events.

This deeper level of engagement benefits both the community and helps to build stronger leadership within the organization. The benefits further attract top talent, solidifying eXp Realty’s global reputation as a leader in real estate.

Qualification Steps to Achieve eXp ICON Agent Status

  • Meet production targets.
  • Engage in cultural commitment.
  • Participate in events.

1. Meet Production Targets

Agents can become ICON by meeting one of the following options after they have capped in a particular year:

  • Do another 20 transactions thereby paying $5000 in capped status transaction fees or
  • Do at least $500,000 Gross Commission Income (GCI) with a minimum of 10 closed transactions + payment of the ICON qualifying fee. ($5000 paid in capped status transaction fees or cash).

Upon meeting these production requirements, agents earn $8000 paid in EXPI stock with a 3 year vesting period.

2. Engage in Cultural Commitment

It deepens the eXp Realty culture and builds camaraderie among agents. By participating in community improvement efforts in various “give back opportunities, agents earn $4000 in company stock.

Engaging in mentorship programs or teaching in eXp World university events are two ways an agent can demonstrate alignment with a company’s desired culture.

This devotion creates new opportunities and fosters trust in the network.

3. Participate in Events

Going to eXp Realty approved events is a requirement for ICON. These events are dense with opportunities for cultural engagement and networking.

Whether it’s an annual conference, regional meet-up, ICON agents earn up to $4000 in EXPI by attending two such events.

Through active engagement, agents will increase their circle of influence and expertise, while connecting with like-minded agents, giving them a competitive advantage.

Common Practices Used by Agents Working Toward ICON Status

Here’s a bullet list of strategies agents can implement to expedite their path to this prestigious status:

  • Optimize sales techniques.
  • Focus on client relationships.
  • Leverage mentorship opportunities.

Optimize Sales Techniques

Personally, I think there’s a lot of untapped potential in improving the effectiveness of sales techniques to increase productivity and close rates. Implementing technology tools to automate sales processes and enhance client experiences is a secret sauce.

By committing to continuous learning and staying attuned to market trends, agents can keep themselves competitive and relevant. Using a CRM system allows for more efficient tracking and management of important client information.

At the same time, digital learning options help you keep your skills honed.

Focus on Client Relationships

Healthy vendor client relationships are critical for building repeat business and earning referrals. By making customer service and customer satisfaction priorities across your firm, you’ll earn impressive client experiences, which will convert into more sales and in turn, larger commissions.

By figuring out ways to customize their services, agents can meet their clients’ needs while keeping communication open and consistent via follow-up.

Leverage Mentorship Opportunities

Guidance from seasoned ICON agents offers priceless knowledge and accelerates career development. Getting involved in these mentorship programs at eXp Realty can unlock you to learning new strategies and perspectives.

This guidance assists newer agents in overcoming obstacles and establishing successful business practices more quickly.

Understanding the eXp ICON Agent Stock Awards

Stock awards are an important part of the ICON Agent honor within eXp Realty. They’re an important part of the overall compensation, serving as a signal of the company’s long-term intention to reward top-producing agents.

These awards reward agents, not only with upfront acknowledgement, but with an equity position in the company’s growth. By aligning their interests with that of eXp Realty, agents are incentivized to help achieve long-term success.

ICON agents can be awarded up to $16,000 in eXp World Holdings common stock. This is made up of an $8,000 Production Award, a $4,000 Cultural Commitment Award, and two $2,000 Event Attendance Awards.

Vesting and Restrictions

The vesting schedule for ICON stock awards requires agents to understand the timeline and any restrictions on sale or transfer. Agents are restricted from selling their ICON stock for a vesting period of at least 3 years.

By not immediately cashing out, agents can share in the upside that comes from growth. By taking this approach, ICON is rewarding loyalty and incentivizing agents to keep their skin in the game and success of the company.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp ICON Agent Status

Is eXp ICON status required to earn 100% commission?
No. Agents earn 100 percent commission after reaching their annual cap regardless of ICON status. ICON is a separate recognition program that reimburses the cap in stock. Agents can earn full commission post-cap without qualifying for ICON.

How does ICON status compare to bonuses at other brokerages?
Unlike cash bonuses or temporary split increases, ICON status converts production performance into company stock. This means rewards are tied to ownership rather than short-term payouts, which changes how income behaves over time compared to traditional brokerage incentive programs.

Who is ICON status best suited for?
ICON status is best suited for agents with consistent transaction volume who regularly reach their annual cap. It is less relevant for low-volume agents and more applicable to producers who want to turn brokerage expenses into long-term equity rather than recurring costs.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp ICON Agent status is designed to reward top production by converting brokerage costs into ownership, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader eXp Realty income structure.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is an eXp ICON Agent?
An eXp ICON Agent is a high-producing eXp Realty agent who meets annual production, cultural, and event participation requirements. ICON status recognizes sustained performance and reimburses the agent’s annual cap in company stock, converting brokerage expenses into long-term ownership rather than cash bonuses.

What are the requirements to qualify for eXp ICON status?
Agents must first cap and then meet additional production thresholds, either through transaction count or gross commission income, along with cultural participation and approved event attendance. Exact requirements are set by eXp Realty and are reviewed annually to ensure consistent performance standards.

Does ICON status change commission splits or caps?
No. ICON status does not alter commission splits or caps. Agents still operate on the standard commission structure. ICON functions as a reimbursement mechanism that returns the annual cap in company stock, rather than modifying how commissions are earned on transactions.

How are eXp ICON stock awards structured?
ICON stock awards are issued in multiple components tied to production, cultural contribution, and event attendance. The combined total can equal the agent’s annual cap. Stock is subject to vesting requirements and cannot be immediately sold or transferred.

Is ICON stock awarded as cash or equity?
ICON rewards are issued as company stock, not cash. This structure aligns agent performance with company growth and is designed to support long-term wealth building rather than short-term income replacement.

Can agents earn stock awards without achieving ICON status?
Yes. Agents can earn stock awards through other production milestones, such as first transaction and capping awards. ICON status is a separate, higher-level recognition that reimburses the cap but does not replace other stock programs.
